name: Almenar de Soria, Ólvega, and Matalebreras Loop
name_original: Ólvega desde Almenar de Soria — circular por Matalebreras
description: This difficult 50.4-mile touring cycling route connects Ólvega, Almenar de Soria, and Matalebreras through the scenic Soria province.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Almenar De Soria features diverse landscapes, from pine forests to the more arid Alhama valley. The region is characterized by expansive agricultural fields that change with the seasons, alongside natural scrubland, lavender, and thyme. These varied terrains provide a range of cycling experiences, often with historical landmarks like the Castle of Almenar de Soria serving as scenic backdrops. Best touring cycling routes around Almenar De Soria The most popular touring cycling route is Santander-Mediterranean Greenway: Valcorba - Ciria, a 30.4 miles (49.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 1 minute to complete. This moderate route follows a greenway, offering varied scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Almenar de Soria, Ólvega, and Matalebreras Loop, a difficult 50.4 miles (81.2 km) path. This route connects multiple towns, providing a challenging ride through the regional landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Ciria – Borobia loop from Ciria, a 23.7 miles (38.1 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 26 minutes. Touring cycling around Almenar De Soria is defined by agricultural fields, pine forests, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Almenar De Soria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 1 review. More than 15 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Almenar De Soria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Almenar de Soria?
A: There are 6 touring cycling routes available around Almenar de Soria, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These include 2 easy, 3 moderate, and 1 difficult route.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Almenar de Soria?
A: The region around Almenar de Soria offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll cycle through pine forests, the more arid Alhama valley, and expansive agricultural fields that transform with the seasons. Expect to see natural scrubland, lavender, and thyme, providing varied and picturesque scenery.

Q: What historical landmarks can I visit along the cycling routes?
A: As you cycle, you can encounter several significant historical and cultural landmarks. The most notable is the well-preserved 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Almenar de Soria&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the 18th-century Hermitage of La Llana and the Gothic-style Church of San Pedro Apóstol. You might also pass through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5519610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciria&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ledesma de Soria Village&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers beautiful vistas. One notable spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6255470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Madero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which provides expansive views of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Almenar de Soria?
A: The touring cycling routes around Almenar de Soria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Over 15 touring cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the opportunity to connect multiple towns.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041638642/almenar-de-soria-olvega-and-matalebreras-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria, Ólvega, and Matalebreras Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 81.2 km ride connecting multiple towns. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31500517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciria – Borobia loop from Ciria&lt;/a&gt;, a 38.1 km trail through rural landscapes.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are easy routes available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gómara Town Center loop from Gómara&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 12.4 km ride, perfect for a relaxed outing. These routes often feature gentle gradients and are suitable for those looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Almenar de Soria?
A: The region's agricultural fields offer picturesque views that transform with the seasons. Spring brings vibrant green waves, summer turns them golden and adorns them with sunflowers. These periods generally offer pleasant weather for cycling, allowing you to enjoy the changing scenery.

Q: Can I find routes that follow greenways or dedicated cycle paths?
A: Yes, the region includes sections of greenways. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041569047/santander-mediterranean-greenway-valcorba-ciria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander-Mediterranean Greenway: Valcorba - Ciria&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 49.0 km route that follows a greenway, offering varied scenery and a dedicated path for cyclists.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through smaller villages or settlements?
A: Many routes in the area connect various towns and villages, allowing you to experience the local culture. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041638642/almenar-de-soria-olvega-and-matalebreras-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria, Ólvega, and Matalebreras Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, linking multiple communities and providing a glimpse into regional life.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on the touring cycling routes?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly depending on the route's difficulty. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gómara Town Center loop from Gómara&lt;/a&gt; have minimal elevation gain (around 90 meters). More challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041638642/almenar-de-soria-olvega-and-matalebreras-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria, Ólvega, and Matalebreras Loop&lt;/a&gt;, can involve over 650 meters of ascent, offering a more demanding ride.
tips: Its castle stands out above all, very well preserved (although small) that has served as the setting for numerous series and films

Matalebreras is a municipality in the province of Soria, in the Moncayo region, in the community of Castilla y León.

It was established as a constitutional municipality with the name of Matalebrera, with 90 homes and 269 neighbors in 1842.

Of its heritage, the Gothic church of San Pedro Apóstol from the 18th century stands out, although it preserves a chapel and the nave from the 16th century. Another unique building is the Mozarabic tower, today in ruins, in addition to three emblazoned houses from the 17th-18th centuries, one of them belonging to the Marquis of Velamazán.

Ólvega is a municipality in Soria in the community of Castilla y León. Its origin dates back to the beginning of the 5th century, although even older archaeological remains have been found from two nearby Celtiberian forts.

It is popularly known as "Niña del Moncayo", for being at the foot of this mountain, and also as "Second Numancia", for the popular revolt of 1474 to avoid rendering vassalage to the Count of Medinaceli.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as difficult. The Soria province features varied terrain, and while some Vías Verdes offer gentle gradients, a route connecting Ólvega, Almenar de Soria, and Matalebreras will likely involve significant elevation changes and a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, requiring good fitness and cycling experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, typical of the Soria province. You can expect a mix of paved roads and unpaved tracks or paths. The region includes mountains, forests, and plains, so you'll encounter diverse surfaces and gradients throughout the journey. Some sections might be on dedicated cycling paths like Vías Verdes, while others could be on gravel or dirt tracks.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route connecting three towns, you have flexibility for parking. You could typically find parking in any of the main towns like Ólvega, Matalebreras, or Almenar de Soria Castle. Look for public parking areas within these settlements. Starting in Ólvega might be convenient due to its size and potential amenities.

faqs.question: What natural features can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the preserved natural beauty of the Soria province. Expect diverse scenery including forests, agricultural lands, and open plains. The route may pass near areas like the Sierra del Madero, offering lush natural surroundings, and provide glimpses into traditional Spanish rural life in one of Spain's least populated provinces.

faqs.question: What historical or cultural points of interest are on the route?
faqs.answer: The route connects several places with historical charm. You'll pass through Almenar de Soria Castle, the settlement of Matalebreras, and the town of Ólvega. Ólvega itself has historical elements like the Ermita de la Virgen de Olmacedo, a 13th-century Romanesque hermitage. The region is rich in heritage, offering a cultural dimension to your cycling journey.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in the Soria province is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). These seasons offer milder temperatures and pleasant weather, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder conditions of winter. This allows for a more comfortable and enjoyable touring cycling experience.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome on many outdoor routes in Spain, especially in rural areas, specific rules can vary. On public roads and unpaved tracks, dogs are usually allowed but should be kept under control, ideally on a leash, especially when passing through villages, agricultural areas, or near livestock. Always be mindful of local regulations and other cyclists.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It primarily utilizes public roads and tracks connecting the towns of Ólvega, Almenar de Soria, and Matalebreras. You are free to explore these areas without special permissions.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for touring cycling in Soria?
faqs.answer: Given the route's 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, you should bring a well-maintained touring bicycle suitable for mixed surfaces, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, and plenty of water. Consider carrying spare tubes, a repair kit, and a pump. Navigation tools are essential as it's a custom route. Layers of clothing are advisable due to potential weather changes, and snacks for energy.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route connects the towns of Ólvega, Almenar de Soria Castle, and Matalebreras, you will find options for food and accommodation in these settlements. Ólvega, being the largest, will likely offer the most choices for restaurants, cafes, and places to stay. It's always a good idea to check opening hours, especially in smaller villages.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route may intersect with or run parallel to other paths. Notably, it covers a small portion of the Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo (Valcorba - Ciria) and the Santander-Mediterráneo. You might also encounter parts of the Camino Jacobeo Castellano-Aragonés: Pozalmuro-Soria and the Camino Natural del Agua Soriano. Camino Antonino.
